Provide notice accordingly for all incidents involving Personally Identifiable Information (PII).

data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Define, classify, and prioritise incidents to help identify the impact and scope of the incident. Establish an incident classification scheme in line with the incident response plan considering regulatory issuances regarding incident management. Ensure that the SOC (Security Operations Centre) performs the following, at a minimum when an information security incident is reported: Assess the information security events using the agreed information security event and incident classification scale. Decide whether the events should be classified as information security incidents. Ensure that the assessment of the incident and the associated decision is forwarded to the CSIRT for confirmation and / or reassessment.

This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

Legal Human Capital for incidents that was originated by an employee. Corporate Communications for managing the communication of incidents that has been revealed to public. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text:
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

To evaluate whether such logging is in place, an entity should periodically scan through its logs and compare them with the asset inventory in order to ensure that each managed item actively connected to the network is periodically generating logs. Analytical programs such as SIM/SEM solutions for reviewing logs can provide value, but the capabilities employed to analyze audit logs are quite extensive, including, importantly, even just a cursory examination by a person. Actual correlation tools can make audit logs far more useful for subsequent manual inspection. Such tools can be quite helpful in identifying subtle attacks. However, these tools are neither a panacea nor a replacement for skilled information security personnel and system administrators. Even with automated log analysis tools, human expertise and intuition are often required to identify and understand attacks. data.policy_text:
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: The CSIRT in coordination with other appropriate teams shall: Develop incident response testing procedures to: Determine the overall effectiveness of its incident response capabilities. Identify potential weaknesses or deficiencies. Ensure that the incident response testing approach simulates pre-defined breach scenarios across the incident response lifecycle including detection, reporting, and recovery to normal operations. Participate in sector, national, and international exercises to further test the organization’s incident response capabilities.

data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Define, classify, and prioritise incidents to help identify the impact and scope of the incident. Establish an incident classification scheme in line with the incident response plan considering regulatory issuances regarding incident management. Ensure that the SOC (Security Operations Centre) performs the following, at a minimum when an information security incident is reported: Assess the information security events using the agreed information security event and incident classification scale. Decide whether the events should be classified as information security incidents. Ensure that the assessment of the incident and the associated decision is forwarded to the CSIRT for confirmation and / or reassessment.

data.policy_text:
This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

data.policy_text: The CSIRT shall: Define, classify, and prioritise incidents to help identify the impact and scope of the incident. Establish an incident classification scheme in line with the incident response plan considering regulatory issuances regarding incident management. Ensure that the SOC (Security Operations Centre) performs the following, at a minimum when an information security incident is reported: Assess the information security events using the agreed information security event and incident classification scale. Decide whether the events should be classified as information security incidents. Ensure that the assessment of the incident and the associated decision is forwarded to the CSIRT for confirmation and / or reassessment.
